weekly.angle: The ontological shift from categorical traits to dimensional functioning, framing Level of Personality Functioning (LPF) as a prime example of how defining 'what personality is' changes how we measure it.
must_cover: The 'Severity vs. Style' distinction as a fundamental redefinition of personality pathology.
The definition of LPF as intrapsychic self and interpersonal functioning.
Positioning LPF as an 'inner essence' model of personality, establishing the baseline intrapsychic view that later sociocultural theories will critique.
avoid: Granular memorization of all specific ICD-11 trait domains and their sub-facets.
Epidemiological statistics and highly specific clinical vignettes that do not serve the core conceptual shift.
Confusing LPF with general psychosocial disability.
grounding: Use the 'how nuts vs. what kind of nuts' analogy from the lecture slides to separate LPF from traits.
Anchor the developmental justification for LPF in Sharp & Wall's use of McAdams' person-as-author model.
Reference Bach & Simonsen for the specific ICD-11 operationalization of self and interpersonal capacities.

per_reading.angle: The theoretical defense of LPF as the core intrapsychic impairment underlying all personality pathology.
must_cover: The distinction between Disease (intrapsychic dysfunction) and Disability (general psychosocial impairment).
The developmental timeline explaining why personality disorders typically onset in adolescence (person-as-author).
avoid: Treating the text as a single empirical experiment rather than a conceptual synthesis and defense of the AMPD.

per_reading.angle: The clinical operationalization of the dimensional model in ICD-11.
must_cover: The division of personality functioning into four self-capacities and four interpersonal capacities.
The use of five trait domains to describe individual stylistic expression.
The Borderline pattern as a specific supplementary code rather than a standalone categorical diagnosis.
avoid: Presenting the five trait domains as independent diagnoses.
Misunderstanding 'personality difficulties' as a mild personality disorder rather than a subclinical state.

per_slide.angle: The practical clinical paradigm shift from a medical 'disease' model to a psychological model of personhood.
must_cover: The 'HOW NUTS vs. WHAT KIND OF NUTS' analogy.
The distinction between primary interpersonal problems (in PDs) and secondary interpersonal problems (in depression/anxiety).
Psychotherapy as the strengthening of personality functions (ego functions).
avoid: Getting lost in the specific DIPP-11 scoring examples without explaining the broader conceptual context.

short.angle: A quick breakdown of the dimensional turn in personality disorders focusing on Severity vs. Style.
must_cover: The shift from categorical diagnoses to dimensional LPF (self/interpersonal functioning) and trait specifiers.
The 'how nuts vs. what kind of nuts' analogy.
avoid: Listing all specific traits or capacities; keep it focused on the conceptual shift.

selected_concepts.why: It is the central construct of the week, representing the dimensional core of personality pathology focusing on intrapsychic self and interpersonal capacities.
Used by Sharp & Wall to explain the developmental necessity of LPF and why personality disorders typically onset during adolescence when narrative identity integrates.
A crucial interpersonal capacity in the ICD-11 model that is often impaired in individuals with personality dysfunction.
Illustrates how the ICD-11 bridges the new dimensional model with existing clinical consensus by using it as a specifier rather than a categorical diagnosis.

selected_tensions.why: Clarifies the two-step diagnostic process in modern dimensional models, separating the core dysfunction of personality (LPF) from its specific trait expression.
Justifies why LPF must focus specifically on intrapsychic self and interpersonal functioning rather than relying on general impairment metrics like occupational success.
Explains why redefining what a personality disorder is (ontology) forces a change in how we diagnose and measure it (epistemology).

grounding_notes: Explicitly use the 'how nuts vs. what kind of nuts' analogy from the lecture slides to explain Severity vs. Style.
Anchor the definition of LPF in Sharp & Wall's intrapsychic focus, contrasting it with general disability.
Ensure the ICD-11 framework (self/interpersonal capacities and trait domains) is attributed to Bach & Simonsen.

source_selection.why: Anchor source providing the theoretical and developmental justification for LPF and the AMPD model.
Major source detailing the specific ICD-11 clinical operationalization of personality functioning and trait domains.
Supporting source providing the crucial pedagogical analogy ('how nuts vs. what kind of nuts') and framing the shift from a medical to a psychological model.
